senators.name: Josh Hawley
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. in history from Stanford University and J.D. from Yale Law School. Clerked for Chief Justice John Roberts — one of the most prestigious legal positions in America. Former associate professor of law at University of Missouri. Author of multiple books. Exceptional academic and legal credentials. However, legislative approach is more populist and ideologically driven than evidence-based. Academic pedigree contrasts with anti-institutional governance style.
experience: Associate Professor of Law, University of Missouri
Law Clerk to Chief Justice John Roberts

senators.name: Eric Schmitt
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. cum laude in political science from Truman State University and J.D. from Saint Louis University (Law Review editor, published author). Career as attorney, state senator, state treasurer, and attorney general. Strong legal analytical credentials but governance approach is more ideologically driven than evidence-based.
education: J.D., Saint Louis University School of Law (Law Review Editor)
B.A. Political Science (cum laude), Truman State University

MO-01.name: Wesley Bell
MO-01.notNerdReason: B.A. in political science and public administration from Lindenwood University and J.D. from University of Missouri-Columbia. Career as public defender, professor, municipal judge, and prosecuting attorney. Legal and academic background provides analytical skills but approach is more community-justice oriented than data-driven.
education: B.A. Political Science and Public Administration, Lindenwood University

MO-02.name: Ann Wagner
MO-02.notNerdReason: B.S.B.A. from University of Missouri with emphasis in logistics. Career in corporate management (Hallmark Cards, Ralston Purina), Republican Party leadership, and diplomacy (Ambassador to Luxembourg). Business and political management background. Solid executive skills but governance approach is more political-operations driven than analytically rigorous.
experience: Management, Hallmark Cards and Ralston Purina

MO-03.name: Bob Onder
MO-03.notNerdReason: B.A. in economics and biology from Washington University in St. Louis, M.D. from Washington University School of Medicine, and J.D. from Saint Louis University School of Law. Rejects climate science and inclusive gender policies.
education: J.D., Saint Louis University School of Law
M.D., Washington University School of Medicine
B.A. Economics and Biology, Washington University in St. Louis

MO-04.name: Mark Alford
MO-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from Mississippi College. Career as TV news anchor for over 25 years. Communications and media background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

MO-05.name: Emanuel Cleaver
MO-05.notNerdReason: B.A. from Prairie View A&M University and M.Div. from Saint Paul School of Theology. Career as pastor and Kansas City mayor. Theological education and pastoral leadership. Deep community engagement but approach is more values-driven than analytically rigorous.
education: M.Div., Saint Paul School of Theology

MO-06.name: Sam Graves
MO-06.notNerdReason: B.A. in agribusiness from University of Missouri. Career as farmer and state legislator. Agriculture background. No distinctly analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
experience: Former Chairman, House Transportation and Infrastructure Committee

MO-07.name: Eric Burlison
MO-07.notNerdReason: B.A. from Southwest Missouri State University and J.D. from University of Missouri School of Law. Career as attorney and state legislator. Legal training provides analytical foundation but governance approach driven by conservative ideology rather than evidence-based analysis.
education: J.D., University of Missouri School of Law

MO-08.name: Jason Smith
MO-08.nerdReason: Two B.S. degrees from University of Missouri (agriculture economics and business administration) and J.D. from Oklahoma City University. Youngest chairman of the House Ways and Means Committee in modern history (since the Civil War). The Ways and Means Committee oversees the most complex fiscal, tax, and trade policy in government. Demonstrates deep quantitative and analytical engagement with federal tax code and economic policy despite young age.
education: B.S. Agriculture Economics, University of Missouri
B.S. Business Administration, University of Missouri
experience: Chairman, House Ways and Means Committee
